title: The Instruction-Following shift
concept: Instruction tuning transforms the raw text generator into a helpful, conversational tool. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Instruction Datasets.'

exercises.prompt: What does a base AI model do before it undergoes instruction tuning?

options.text: It autocompletes text based on patterns.
It browses the live web in real time.

exercises.explain: Raw models are trained to autocomplete text, which doesn't make them good assistants until they are specifically tuned.

cards.title: From Auto-Complete to Assistant
cards.body: Have you ever wondered why early AI models just repeated your questions, while modern AIs actually answer them like helpful assistants? That change is called the instruction-following shift!

cards.title: What is Instruction Tuning?
cards.body: Instruction Tuning is the process of training a base AI model using pairs of instructions and their correct responses. This teaches the model to act as an assistant that answers prompts, rather than just a machine that auto-completes sentences.

cards.title: The Wild Horse vs. The Trained Guide
cards.body: Imagine a raw text generator as a wild horse that just runs in whatever direction it starts. Instruction tuning is like training that horse to carry a rider, follow commands, and stop when you say 'halt'. It turns wild energy into a cooperative partner.

cards.title: Before vs. After Tuning
cards.body: Before tuning, if you type: 'Write a recipe for chocolate cake', the model might respond with: 'Write a recipe for vanilla cookies next'. After instruction tuning, it actually lists the ingredients and steps to bake the cake!

cards.title: Myth: Bigger Models Chat Naturally
cards.body: Many believe that models naturally know how to chat because they are big. In reality, without explicit instruction tuning, even the biggest models would just write random web articles instead of answering your questions.

cards.title: The Shift in Action
cards.body: See how the behavior of the model changes before and after the instruction-following training.

right.text: Responds directly to prompts as a helpful assistant.

left.text: Predicts the next word to autocomplete internet text.

exercises.prompt: What is a raw, untuned AI model most likely to do when you ask it a question?

options.text: Give you a direct, polite answer.
Complete the sentence or repeat the question.
Write a fully functioning video game.

exercises.explain: Without instruction tuning, a base model's only goal is to predict what words come next, which often means repeating or extending the prompt instead of answering it.

exercises.prompt: Instruction tuning is the key step that turns a simple text predictor into a helpful chatbot assistant.
exercises.explain: Instruction tuning is what teaches the AI to act like a conversational partner instead of just a document auto-completer.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of this training shift.
exercises.sentence: To teach AI how to help us, developers train them using a dataset of instructions and answers.
exercises.explain: Instruction tuning requires pairs of human instructions and correct answers to teach the AI how to behave.

exercises.prompt: Which of these conversations shows a model that has successfully undergone the Instruction-Following shift?

options.text: User: "Why is the sky blue?" AI: "...and why is grass green? Click here to find out!"
User: "Make a list of three red fruits." AI: "1. Apple, 2. Strawberry, 3. Cherry."
User: "Help me write a song." AI: "Help me write a song. Help me write a book. Help me write a play."

exercises.explain: The prompt 'Make a list of three red fruits' is a direct command, and the response listing Apples, Strawberries, and Cherries is a perfect example of following instructions. The others are just autocomplete patterns.

exercises.prompt: Why does the Instruction-Following shift make AI models safer and more useful?

options.text: It trains the model to copy grammar mistakes.
It aligns the model's responses to match human commands.
It speeds up the hardware chip inside the computer.
It prevents the model from generating any text at all.

exercises.explain: By shifting from predicting words to following instructions, LLMs became the chatbots we know today. Next up: Instruction Datasets.
